skill.name: senior-computer-vision
skill.description: Computer vision guidance for image processing, object detection, segmentation, video analysis, model training, inference optimization, and production deployment.
skill.summary: Build and optimize production computer vision systems for images, video, and real-time inference.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: Reviewed 55 static findings across 8 files. All reviewed findings are false positives caused by computer vision terms, markdown command examples, and generic security documentation. No prompt injection, network exfiltration, credential access, or malicious execution was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: SAM Computer Vision Term
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SAM as the Windows SAM database. In context, SAM refers to the Segment Anything Model in computer vision descriptions and examples. No Windows credential database access was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced text uses SAM alongside PyTorch, YOLO, segmentation, and ViT. That semantic context confirms a computer vision model, not a Windows credential store.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keywords
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography patterns, but the referenced files contain documentation terms such as algorithms, encryption, scalability, and SAM. The Python scripts contain argparse descriptions at the flagged line and no cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found documentation prose and CLI metadata, not MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or custom cryptographic code. The detected terms do not create a security behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Command Examples
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged shell or Ruby backtick execution in SKILL.md. The locations are markdown code fences and inline file references that document example commands. They are not executable code paths in the skill runtime.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious characters occur inside markdown formatting. No Ruby code, shell interpolation, subprocess call, or automatic command execution is present at those locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance Keywords
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged network reconnaissance in the reference documents. The cited lines discuss resource awareness for performance design, not scanning hosts, ports, services, or networks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding section is performance design and lists efficient algorithms, resource awareness, caching, and batch processing. There is no network API or reconnaissance workflow.

content.value_statement: Computer vision projects often fail when model choices, data quality, and deployment constraints are handled separately. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through practical architecture, training, optimization, and production planning.
actual_capabilities: Plan object detection systems using YOLO, Faster R-CNN, and related architectures.
Design segmentation workflows for semantic, instance, and prompt-based segmentation tasks.
Explain dataset preparation, annotation quality checks, augmentation, and validation strategy.
Guide inference optimization with batching, quantization, ONNX, TensorRT, and hardware-aware profiling.
Outline production deployment patterns with monitoring, drift checks, scaling, and incident response.
Provide CLI templates for training, inference optimization, and dataset pipeline workflows.
limitations: The included Python scripts are scaffold templates and do not train real models by themselves.
Users must provide datasets, labels, model weights, compute resources, and deployment credentials.
The skill gives guidance but does not verify visual outputs without access to user images or videos.
Specialized 3D reconstruction, LiDAR fusion, and medical imaging require domain review.

use_cases.title: Train a custom detector
use_cases.description: Plan dataset formatting, model selection, training stages, metrics, and export steps for custom object detection.

use_cases.title: Improve vision model performance
use_cases.description: Diagnose accuracy, latency, class imbalance, and annotation issues in image or video pipelines.

use_cases.title: Deploy real-time inference
use_cases.description: Design serving, scaling, monitoring, and rollback patterns for production computer vision services.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a starting architecture
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a new project
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose a computer vision architecture for [TASK]. My data is [DATA_DESCRIPTION], and my target device is [DEVICE].

prompt_templates.title: Plan dataset preparation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a dataset preparation plan for [DETECTION_OR_SEGMENTATION_TASK]. Include annotation format, validation checks, augmentation, and split strategy.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize inference latency
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my inference pipeline for [MODEL_NAME]. Current latency is [LATENCY], hardware is [HARDWARE], and accuracy target is [METRIC].

prompt_templates.title: Design production deployment
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a production computer vision service for [USE_CASE]. Include model serving, monitoring, drift detection, scaling, security, and rollback strategy.

output_examples.input: I need to detect defects on a manufacturing line with limited labeled images.
output: Start with transfer learning from a lightweight detector and freeze early layers first.
Audit labels for missing boxes, inconsistent classes, and poor edge cases before training.
Use targeted augmentation for lighting, blur, rotation, and background variation.
Track precision, recall, false rejects, and false accepts by defect type.
Deploy a confidence review queue for uncertain predictions during rollout.

output_examples.input: My segmentation model is accurate but too slow for live video.
output: Profile preprocessing, inference, and postprocessing separately before changing the model.
Test a smaller backbone, reduced input size, mixed precision, and batch scheduling.
Export through ONNX or TensorRT when the target hardware supports it.
Measure accuracy loss on real video clips, not only still images.
Use asynchronous frame handling when occasional stale predictions are acceptable.

output_examples.input: We need a production plan for an image moderation model.
output: Define class taxonomy, review thresholds, appeal paths, and human escalation rules.
Add monitoring for input drift, class distribution changes, latency, and review backlog.
Separate model serving from policy configuration so thresholds can change safely.
Log decisions with privacy controls and retention limits.
Run shadow tests before replacing the current moderation workflow.

best_practices: Start from pretrained models and document why each architecture matches the task, data, and deployment target.
Validate annotation quality before training because label errors can dominate model performance.
Measure latency, accuracy, throughput, and drift with production-like inputs before release.
anti_patterns: Training from scratch when pretrained vision models would reduce data, cost, and risk.
Optimizing only model inference while ignoring preprocessing, postprocessing, and data transfer time.
Deploying computer vision systems without monitoring data drift, failure cases, and human review paths.

faq.question: What computer vision tasks does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers image processing, object detection, segmentation, video analysis, model training, inference optimization, and production deployment.

faq.question: Does this skill include working model code?
faq.answer: It includes scaffold scripts and guidance. Users must add real training logic, datasets, labels, and model weights.

faq.question: Can it help with YOLO or OpenCV projects?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill discusses YOLO, OpenCV, PyTorch, TensorFlow, detection workflows, and real-time inference tradeoffs.

faq.question: Can it review my existing pipeline?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide architecture details, metrics, hardware, data constraints, and target outcomes for a focused review.

faq.question: Does it run training automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides plans, commands, and script templates, but execution depends on the user environment.

faq.question: What should I prepare before using it?
faq.answer: Prepare task goals, sample data details, label format, target hardware, latency goals, and accuracy metrics.
